skill.name: full-output-enforcement
skill.description: Guides AI assistants to produce complete, unabridged outputs instead of placeholders, skipped sections, or shortened implementations.
skill.summary: Enforces complete AI responses and clean continuation points for long outputs.
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: The static external command findings are false positives from Markdown inline code and a fenced text example in SKILL.md. The weak cryptography findings are also false positives; no cryptographic code, shell execution, network access, filesystem access, or prompt-injection attempt was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md line 16 uses inline backticks to list banned placeholder text, and lines 37-39 show a literal pause marker in a fenced block. These are documentation examples, not Ruby or shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched content is Markdown formatting and static text. No executable Ruby, shell invocation, command arguments, or user-input execution path exists in the file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Prose
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. Static findings at SKILL.md lines 3, 20, and 48 do not reference MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic operation. The text describes output completeness requirements.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are natural-language instructions about complete outputs. I found no cryptographic API, algorithm selection, hashing code, or security-sensitive data handling.

content.value_statement: AI assistants often shorten long answers, skip repeated sections, or leave placeholder code. This skill sets strict completion rules and clean continuation behavior for full responses.
actual_capabilities: Requires the assistant to count requested deliverables before answering.
Blocks placeholder phrases that replace requested work.
Encourages complete code, prose, and section delivery.
Defines a pause marker for long outputs near token limits.
Directs continuation from the exact next unfinished section.
Adds a final completeness check before the response.
limitations: It cannot increase the model context window or token limit.
It does not validate whether generated code actually runs.
It may produce longer responses than a user needs.
It depends on the assistant following the skill instructions.

use_cases.title: Full File Generation
use_cases.description: Generate complete files without placeholder comments or omitted middle sections.

use_cases.title: Complete Documentation Drafts
use_cases.description: Produce every requested section in a guide, runbook, or specification.

use_cases.title: Long Task Continuation
use_cases.description: Pause at clean breakpoints and continue without repeating prior content.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Complete Answer
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a complete response.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use full-output-enforcement. Answer the request completely, and do not use placeholders or shortened sections.

prompt_templates.title: Write Every Requested Section
prompt_templates.prompt: Use full-output-enforcement. Count each requested section first, then deliver every section in full.

prompt_templates.title: Create Full Implementation Files
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced coding task with multiple files.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use full-output-enforcement. Generate every requested file completely, with runnable code instead of placeholder comments.

prompt_templates.title: Handle Token Limits Cleanly
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced long-form task that may exceed one response.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use full-output-enforcement. If the response reaches the token limit, stop at a clean breakpoint and provide the required pause marker.

output_examples.input: Create five complete onboarding checklist sections.
output_examples.output: The assistant provides all five sections with finished checklist items, not one sample section and a note to repeat it.

output_examples.input: Write a complete implementation plan for three services.
output_examples.output: The assistant covers each service fully, verifies the requested count, and avoids shortened middle sections.

output_examples.input: Draft a long migration guide that may exceed one response.
output_examples.output: The assistant writes until a clean section boundary, then pauses with a precise continuation point.

best_practices: Use this skill when completeness matters more than brevity.
State the exact number of files, sections, or deliverables you expect.
Ask for continuation when the assistant uses the pause marker.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for quick summaries or short exploratory answers.
Do not request complete output when requirements are still unclear.
Do not treat complete generation as proof that code is tested.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands?
faq.answer: No. It is a Markdown instruction file and contains no executable scripts.

faq.question: Can it bypass model token limits?
faq.answer: No. It only defines how to pause and continue long outputs.

faq.question: Will it make responses longer?
faq.answer: Yes. It prioritizes complete delivery over concise answers.

faq.question: Is it useful for code generation?
faq.answer: Yes. It discourages placeholder comments and incomplete implementation sketches.

faq.question: Does it test generated code?
faq.answer: No. It improves output completeness but does not execute or verify code.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: It lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
